mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS momentum are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and falling industrial production (INDPRO) signal stagflationary supply constraints that squeeze profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of CPIAUCSL and INDPRO fails to produce a rank correlation (Rank IC) below -0.03 with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions marked by elevated interest rates, a decline in housing starts (HOUST) foreshadows a contraction in rate-sensitive durable demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, declining HOUST is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns after INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, labor market deterioration triggers recessionary earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a 0.3pp rise in UNRATE over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) concurrent with falling unemployment (UNRATE) confirms a broad-based demand expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 with 5-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above 2% annualized signals resilient aggregate demand that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.
